data.title: Stallion Uranium increase private placement to raise C$1.45M
European markets back in red with global sell-off sentiment as tariffs took effect
data.description:
London (UKX) -2.15% to 7,740. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -2.29% to 19,815.

France (CAC:IND) -2.28% to 6,938.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 2.98% lower to 472.42, reversing gains from the previous as trade tensions intensified after Trump's sweeping tariffs, including a cumulative 104% levy on Chinese imports. Losses were broad-based, with all sectors in negative territory. Pharmaceuticals, oil & gas, basic resources, and real estate led the declines. Meanwhile, Spain's Prime Minister, Pedro Sánchez, called on the European Union to review its relationship with China.

European natural gas futures hit its lowest level since September as tariffs fueled fears of a global economic slowdown and weaker energy demand.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 12 basis points at 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point at 2.62%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points to 4.67%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: M&A Snapshot: KKR, Stonepeak to acquire UK's Assura in $2.06 billion all-cash deal
Spain calls on EU to rethink relations with China amid Trump's sweeping tariffs
Microsoft reclaims title of world's most valuable public company after Apple selloff
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has reclaimed the title of the world's most valuable publicly-listed company, following Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) four-day losing streak amid the broader stock market meltdown in the aftermath of President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs.

The market capitalization of Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stood at $2.64T as of Tuesday's close, while that of Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) totaled $2.59T.

While Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) shares have fallen 7% over the last four trading sessions, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) saw a steeper decline of 23% in the same period. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell 13% since Trump announced his tariff plan.  

This is because of the iPhone maker's reliance on China for a significant portion of its manufacturing needs. It also manufactures devices in other countries facing higher tariff rates, including India, Vietnam and Brazil. 

Jefferies analysts in a recent note said Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is "more insulated" from macro pressures and continues to view the stock as "the safest large-cap investment option."

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) briefly held the title of the most valuable public company early last year, but had since been overtaken by Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA), which is now at No. 3 with a market cap of $2.35T.

data.title: Heathrow AOC, Collins Aerospace extend deal for smoother passenger flow
data.description:
Collins Aerospace, an RTX business (NYSE:RTX), has secured a six-year renewal pact with Heathrow Airline Operators' Committee (AOC) to continue providing its ARINC cMUSE solution across all four London-Heathrow Airport terminals.

The system serves over 80 airlines, enabling dynamic allocation of check-in desks and boarding gates based on real-time demand.

The common-use passenger processing system has been operational at Heathrow since 1999, supporting one of the world's busiest airports, which handled 83.9 million passengers in 2024. The airport currently features more than 1,500 common-use workstations, making it Collins' largest CUPPS installation globally.

Collins Aerospace and Heathrow AOC will collaborate for five years to explore cloud and mobile technologies, including the ARINC cMUSE system, which offers scalability and 24/7 global support.

data.title: UK's Assura agrees to $2 billion takeover offer by KKR-Stonepeak
data.description:
Funds managed by KKR (NYSE:KKR) and Stonepeak Partners have agreed to buy UK-based healthcare property investor and developer Assura (OTCPK:ARSSF) for £1.6 billion ($2.1 billion) in cash.

The U.K.-listed company said Wednesday that it reached an agreement with Sana Bidco, a newly formed company wholly owned by funds advised by both KKR and Stonepeak.

The offer for Assura’s (OTCPK:ARSSF) shareholders stands at 49.4 pence per share, which includes a 48.56 pence cash consideration from Bidco and a 0.84 pence quarterly interim dividend announced on February 18, 2025, and due to be paid today. 

This offer represents a premium of about 31.9% over the closing price of Assura shares on February 13.

KKR and Stonepeak believe that Assura plays a growing role in the provision of critical healthcare infrastructure in the UK and Ireland.  

Earlier on Wednesday, Assura rejected a revised takeover bid of 1.48 billion pounds from Primary Health Properties (OTCPK:PHPRF).

data.title: Trump says TSMC would face 100% tax if it doesn't build in U.S.
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said that he warned Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (NYSE:TSM) that it would face a tax of up to 100% if it did not build its factories in the U.S. 

Speaking at a Republican National Congressional Committee event, Trump criticized former President Joe Biden's administration for giving a $6.6 billion subsidy for TSMC's Phoenix, Arizona operations, arguing that semiconductor companies do not need such financial support.

"TSMC, I gave them no money ... all I did was say, if you don't build your plant here, you're going to pay a big tax," Trump said.

TSMC declined to comment in an email to Seeking Alpha.

Last month, TSMC (NYSE:TSM) announced plans to invest $100 billion in the U.S., including building five additional chip facilities.

Meanwhile, Taiwan is seeking a swift resolution to U.S.-imposed 32% tariffs. Taiwan responded to such brutal levies with an offer of zero tariffs, and an offer to invest and buy more from the U.S.

Taiwan-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWT), (NYSEARCA:FLTW).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SBM Offshore signs $400 million sale and leaseback agreement for FPSO Cidade de Paraty
data.description:
• SBM Offshore (OTCPK:SBFFF) said it has signed a non-recourse sale and leaseback financing agreement for FPSO Cidade de Paraty for the total amount of $400 million and with a tenor of 8 years.
  • The transaction is expected to be completed before the end of April 2025.
  • FPSO Cidade de Paraty is owned by a special purpose company owned by affiliated companies of SBM Offshore (63.125%) and its partners (36.875%).
  • Under the terms of the agreement, the special purpose company will transfer the ownership to four Chinese leasing companies.
  • SBM Offshore (OTCPK:SBFFF) and its partners continue to operate and maintain the asset until the end of the initial charter and operate contracts for the remaining period of 8.5 years.
